name: Gate3587Bug
description:
Issue #3587 demo. Demonstrates the parked-work-never-recovers bug: if the Deacon session dies after `bd gate check` closes a gate but before `gt gate wake <id>` runs, parked polecats never receive wake notification and wait indefinitely.

Topology (post P1+P2 hardening):
  - Pol (Polecat): receives Park (from WorkLoop), transitions Absent -> Parked AND atomically writes to ParkedFile. Receives Wake on WAKE_IN to transition Parked -> Working.
  - Iss (Issue): bound for Polecat's ISSUE_OBSERVE; stays Open throughout (no SLING).
  - Pf (ParkedFile): persists the parked-state on Polecat's transition. Models parked-<agent>.json on disk.
  - Wl (WorkLoop): observes Gate; when Gate=Open, fires Park to Polecat. Models the polecat work loop encountering a gate-blocking step.
  - Gt (Gate): starts Open; transitions to Closed when Deacon sends Close.
  - Dec (Deacon): runs the two-step protocol -- close gate, then send wake. Idle -> ClosingGate guarded on (Polecat=Parked AND Gate=Open).
  - Rpr (Reaper): nondeterministically sends Kill to Deacon. Models external session death (SIGTERM, OOM, pane close).

Critical bug interleaving: Deacon transitions Idle -> ClosingGate -> WakeReady (observes Gate=Closed). Reaper sends Kill before Deacon sends Wake. Deacon transitions WakeReady -> Crashed. Gate is Closed, no Wake in flight, Polecat stays Parked. PolecatEventuallyWorking liveness property fails.

LIMITATIONS (what the model does and does not capture):
  - Multi-shot prime not modeled: real `gt prime` fires every agent startup; the modeled PrimeRecovery (in fix spec) is single-shot. Conservative under-approximation -- the fix is at least as effective as modeled.
  - Issue is decorative: bound to satisfy Polecat.ISSUE_OBSERVE; stays Open. Not exercised.
  - Crash window position: Deacon only consumes Kill at WakeReady. Kill arriving in other Deacon states sits in flight; no crash happens. The bug-window is the WakeReady state specifically per the issue body, so this is a focused model rather than a comprehensive crash study.
  - Bug shape is liveness, not safety: a state-predicate 'no stuck combination' invariant cannot distinguish bug from fix because the combination is briefly reachable in both (transient before PrimeRecovery fires in the fix). Liveness is the natural property here.

Real sources: cmd/bd/gate.go:818 closeGate(), internal/cmd/gate.go:84 gt gate wake, internal/cmd/prime.go (where the proposed checkParkedWork() would land).

Companion specs:
  - gate-3587-fix.kinner.json -- adds PrimeRecovery for the proposed self-heal
  - gate-3587-multi-bug.kinner.json / multi-fix -- 2 polecat/gate pairs, verifies bug compounds linearly and fix scales
  - gate-3587-successor-bug.kinner.json / successor-fix -- 2 Deacons, verifies that a successor session can't recover (closed gate is invisible per `bd gate check`'s status=open filter)

properties.description: Liveness: under fairness, the parked polecat eventually reaches Working. Fails in this bug spec because Deacon's WakeReady -> Crashed branch leaves Polecat without a wake.
